group_heading: Answer in Six or More Sentences

subject: english
text:
“You say you own me; I wait”
A) Who is the speaker?
B) Who does ‘you’ refer to?
C) What does the line tell about the speaker?

subject: english
text:
“I wait with muddy holes”
A) Name the poem from where this extract is taken?
B) Who does ‘I’ refer to?
C) What does ‘muddy holes’ mean?

subject: english
text:
“Then someone tickles me, plant life ________ fruit________grass…trees”
A) What does tickle mean in the context?
B) What is the mood of the speaker in the statement?
C) Who is the ‘someone’ mentioned in the line?

subject: english
text:
“You come with guns a chain link necklace chokes me now”.
A) Why do they come with guns?
B) What is the feeling of the speaker?
C) What does ‘chain-link necklace’ mean in the context?

subject: english
text:
King Ashoka was a kind, wise and righteous ruler. He spent all his time for thinking of the welfare of
his subjects. He had a strong desire to make his subjects happy. His subjects could meet him at any time
and in any place. He had planted trees on either side of the roads; he had dug wells by the roadside; he
had built rest houses for both men and animals. He was respected by everyone.
A) How did Ashoka usually spend his time?
B) Mention any two things that Ashoka did for the welfare of his subjects.
